title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to OpsPal's Salesforce RevOps assessment capabilities, including automatic revenue field discovery and sales process analysis. We also introduced Windows PowerShell support for easier onboarding and added comprehensive security scanning to protect your data. Platform stability was enhanced with fixes to routing and permission handling across all integrations.

entries.headline: Automated weekly release notes now available
details: Release notes are now automatically generated and updated daily with the latest platform improvements and fixes

entries.headline: Salesforce permission workflows now complete properly
Simple Salesforce record lookups now work faster
details: Quick queries for single records or small result sets now run without additional processing overhead

entries.headline: Windows setup now installs required tools automatically
details: Windows users can now get OpsPal running with automatic installation of Git, Node.js, and other dependencies

entries.headline: Better error detection when Salesforce connections fail
details: OpsPal now detects and reports connection issues more clearly when working with Salesforce orgs

entries.headline: Windows PowerShell installation script works reliably
details: Fixed compatibility issues that prevented the Windows setup script from running correctly in all environments

entries.headline: Read-only Salesforce queries now work on production orgs
details: You can now safely run reporting queries against production Salesforce environments without restrictions

entries.headline: Revenue assessment results now save properly for future reference
Brand gallery and templates can now be customized safely
details: You can now customize OpsPal's templates and branding elements without losing changes during platform updates

entries.headline: RevOps assessment now finds revenue fields and sales stages automatically
details: OpsPal automatically discovers your organization's revenue tracking fields and sales process stages for more accurate assessments

entries.headline: Windows setup prefers Git Bash for better compatibility
Setup process now installs required packages automatically
Windows PowerShell bootstrap available for easy setup
Setup experience improved with better guidance and detection
Salesforce deployment validation prevents common issues
details: New validation tools catch picklist, record type, and field history issues before they cause deployment failures

entries.headline: Secret scanning now prevents accidental credential commits
Continuous security scanning now monitors all code changes
Hardcoded credentials removed from all platform files
